job_details.insured_name: Viridian Management | Ramo Flat Apartment: Unit #10

notes:
** Post Demo Matterport linked

Client Phil -

WeScope - Ready for cleaning estimate. 

This was a bio, with three small areas of blood stains on the carpet and a couple drops on the dining room table, chair and pantry door. 

Smell was almost non-existent but I still ran a negative air out the back door for the day. 

Only 1 technician (one heavy duty PPE), no floor protection. 

Consider 60 SF of the living room carpet and pad as category 3 with 60 SF of detailed hepa vacuuming. 

Light floor clean on all remaining living, hallway and master bedroom areas. 

Fog antimicrobial throughout the unit, include a clean table, clean chair and clean one side of door line item. 

Include move out reset, normal size room for both the living room and master bedroom. 

One truckload worth of debris. 

Include 130 additional miles as the home was 190 miles roundtrip from the office with a standard roundtrip servicing range of 60 miles. 

Can also include an hour of supervisory cleaning time.

***Scope Only***

- Scope - 
Client Casey

Taking bid and putting into XM8 pricing

Bid attached

Attached above is the sub bid that I need put into XM8 line items, I know that it will probably come out under the sub bid and that Is ok. Can you get this done as soon as possible.

** Added ESX to PD
** Photos in DASH

--------------------------

- Client Philip -

WeScope - Can you work out a preliminary remediation estimate for us on this one? Forrest and I will need to go through and review as it's a complex job.

Couldn't Matterport due to the conditions so I took several photos and then sketched it out on paper. Just got the sketch finished on Xactimate (currently in the cloud).

Metal building and masonry building are connected and have similar issues with staining and mold growth on walls and ceilings in the corners. We will need to treat the entire area in the metal building (primarily focus will be covering the sluff units and then pressure washing off a majority of the ceiling, walls and i-beams) whereas the masonry room the primary focus is the balcony area with extensive growth).

We are working with the plant manager on this and will need some significant overhead included. They let us know that they'd be shutting off lights for us to properly treat the ceiling, so we will need temporary lights on-site. Will likely need to rent a 45' extendable boom lift for a weeks' worth of time for the metal areas that we can't properly extend to and spray from the upper walkway. Significant cleaning and wipe down will be needed on the balconies, walk-ways and stairs. Include significant PPE for 3 technicians for 2 weeks' worth of remediation.

**Client advised sketch without EV for now. I advised we will sketch but SF will require the EV report - O.

**SKETCH**
The shed is 10x12. 

-Client Carson-
Inspected job. The shed is 10x12. 3 tab shingles needing replaced on the full shed. As well as 3 sheets of 4x8 3/4" decking board. Photo of shed is uploaded into the inspection album.
This is a non program job

Hey Wescope,

I’ve gotten most of the estimate wrapped up — all the mold remediation items have been added. This is currently a single estimate, and just a heads-up: O&P should be removed from all mold-related line items and only applied to the repair work.

The only thing left to add is the exterior scope. I won’t have time to get back to this tomorrow, so I’m hoping you can finish it up for me. I’ll need it back by end of day tomorrow so I can send it over to the insured.

Here’s what still needs to be included for the exterior:

Replacement of soffits (2 ft wide)

Full replacement of gutters around the house

Replace all exterior window casings

I don’t have a full sketch of the exterior, but I’ve got the interior Matterport and a sky view showing the roof dimensions — that should be enough to finish things out.

If you can have this to me by mid-afternoon, that would be perfect. Thanks again.
